response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and communication skills, they fundamentally lack the mandatory technical qualifications. Their C++ experience is limited to approximately 6–8 months a decade ago with no subsequent work in the language or on Windows platforms. They have zero experience in fixed income, capital markets, investment banking, or financial services domains. Their career trajectory over the past decade has been firmly in web and full-stack JavaScript/TypeScript development, moving away from systems programming. The combination of stale C++ skills, missing domain expertise, and unclear SQL Server/Oracle proficiency makes this a poor fit for a mid-senior capital markets role.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software engineering practices including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code craftsmanship across multiple roles
Proven ability to communicate technical concepts effectively through conference talks, workshops, and public projects
Adaptable engineer who has worked across diverse technical domains and programming languages, suggesting foundational problem-solving skills
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (approximately 6–8 months at National Instruments) and dormant for the past ~10 years; career has pivoted to JavaScript/TypeScript full-stack development
Entirely lacking the mandatory financial domain expertise—no experience in fixed income, capital markets, investment banking, or financial services
No evidence of Windows platform C++ development, SQL Server, or Oracle experience; most recent work uses MongoDB and general SQL rather than enterprise database systems

key_factors.factor: Current C++ expertise (5+ years, actively used)
Financial domain knowledge (fixed income or capital markets)
Windows platform and SQL Server/Oracle experience
